PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Advanced From Jan-20-21 To Dec-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-20-303561 Estate of Harry James Owens and PES-20-304054 Estate of Yvonne Hendricks Owens.

Appearances: Emily Nashban appeared by zoom for Youlonda Wayne (Petitioner).

Given the filing of Yvonne's petition, the Court asks if Harry's petition can come off calendar. Ms. Nashban responded "yes". The Court stated that the issue is whether both Sister-in-Laws intended the gift to be separate property, or community property, that absent everything else the assumption is that it is community property, noted that notice was given and there are no objections, and stated that it intends to grant Yvonne's petition. Ms. Nashban stated that it was her understanding that the gift would not have been made to Yvonne if she had not been married to Harry and that it was a family property. The Spousal Property Petition for Harry Owens is off calendar. The Spousal Property Petition for Yvonne Owens is granted.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Ex Parte Application To Advance Hearing Date For Spousal Property Petition
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Determine Validity Of The Assignments
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-29-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emily Nashban appeared by zoom for Youlonda Owens Wayne (Petitioner).
John Boessenecker appeared by zoom for W.C. Cox & Co.

The Court stated that it thinks the parties' positions are that there is no objection to Ms. Wayne's' petition and that it requires bond in the amount of $640,000.00. Ms. Nashban and Mr. Boessenecker indicated that the Court is correct. Ms. Nashban stated that her Client is fully bondable and that it is her understanding that the two Sisters are still standing on their declaration. Mr. Boessenecker agrees. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $648,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for February 28, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of a petition to determine validity of the assignments is set for September 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Boessenecker is to have his petition filed by September 16, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
